# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

version: '3'
services:
    postgres_db1: # assuming the name of your database instance will be dbname in a real case scenario you need to use environment variable here if necessary 
        image: "postgres"  
        restart: always        
        command: --config-file=/etc/postgresql/conf.d/pg_hba_enablesegurityfailure.sql # this file will contain authentication rules set up by a yaml for each user role and host in pg configuration files  /docker volume where your PostgreSQL data would be stored
        volumes:  
            - ./data:/var/lib/postgresql/data   		# provide local directory as the docker runtime has its own db storage that is not accessible from outside. this can also act as a shared folder for other services or even databases if needed
